label: SES-151 / CNV-053 close-out — PI-123 Architecture phase: the unified multi-engagement DB migration design + decomposition. Produced pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md (D1-D11) and dogfooded the ADO substrate: dispatched PI-123 (Draft -> In Progress), decomposed it into the six phase Workstreams (WSK-008..013), scoped + executed + completed the Architecture Workstream (WSK-008) via Work Task WTK-025. Records the architecture's defining strategic choices as DEC-375. Addresses (does NOT resolve) PI-123 — resolution comes at Deployment. session.session_executive_summary: PI-123 Architecture/design pass (06-01-26) for the unified multi-engagement-DB migration (PRJ-019), which PI-122 is blocked_by. Produced pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md settling D1-D11 — most consequentially the identifier-collision fix (composite (engagement_id, identifier) keying, no history renumbered) and the central ContextVar + do_orm_execute + before_flush access-layer rewrite chosen over threaded params — plus the six-phase decomposition. Also dogfooded the ADO substrate end-to-end: dispatched PI-123 (Draft -> In Progress), decomposed it (WSK-008..013), and scoped/executed/completed the Architecture Workstream (WSK-008) via Work Task WTK-025, unblocking Development. session.session_description: Architecture/design pass for PI-123 (Unified multi-engagement DB migration, PRJ-019). Deliverable: pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md (committed 8082535), the Architecture-phase design + decomposition that the build phases consume, grounded in the live code (access/db.py, config.py, runtime/engagement_routing.py, access/meta_db.py, _helpers.py, the refs/identifier_reservations models). It settles D1-D11 (see executive summary) — most consequentially the identifier-collision fix (composite (engagement_id, identifier) keying, both live engagements' sequences preserved untouched) and the access-layer routing rewrite (a central ContextVar + do_orm_execute read-filter + before_flush stamp, with a cross-engagement leak-test as the regression net, chosen over threading an engagement_id parameter through every repository to confine the blast radius and make the default-correct rather than default-dangerous). It realizes what the v0.5 multi-engagement architecture parked as PI-017/DEC-081 (its committed prototype-to-production migration). This session ALSO dogfooded the ADO substrate (PI-114) to model itself: POST /planning-items/PI-123/dispatch (Draft -> In Progress); POST /planning-items/PI-123/decompose (six phase Workstreams WSK-008 Architecture .. WSK-013 Deployment, serial blocked_by); POST /workstreams/WSK-008/scope (one storage-area Work Task WTK-025 representing the design, Planned -> Scoping -> Ready); start-execution (Ready -> In Progress, WTK-025 readied); claim + WTK-025 Ready -> Claimed -> In Progress -> Complete; complete-phase (WSK-008 -> Complete). Net governance: SES-151, CNV-053, DEC-375, the design-doc commit, the session/conversation membership + works-work-task + follows-from edges, and an addresses edge to PI-123 (non-resolving — only the Architecture phase is done). Follows from SES-150.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Architecture phase — unified multi-engagement DB design + decomposition
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Architecture phase — unified multi-engagement DB design + decomposition

Architecture-phase deliverable for PI-123 (PRJ-019), the unified multi-tenant DB migration that PI-122 (Agent Profile Registry) is blocked_by. Settles D1-D11 (meta-fold; engagement_id discriminator; composite-identifier collision fix preserving per-engagement sequences; refs scoping; central ContextVar + do_orm_execute read-filter + before_flush stamp routing rewrite; X-Engagement header with current_engagement.json marker default; scoped snapshot; single Alembic chain; one-shot idempotent consolidation of the live CRMBUILDER.db + CBM.db; single-file ops; registry hooks for PI-122) and decomposes PI-123 into the six ADO phase Workstreams.

decisions.title: PI-123 unified multi-engagement DB architecture — meta-fold, engagement_id discriminator with composite-identifier keying, and a central-filter access-layer rewrite
decisions.context: PI-123 (PRJ-019) replaces CRMBuilder v2's per-engagement-DB-files model (one SQLite file per engagement under data/engagements/, a separate meta DB for the engagement registry, an Alembic chain each, single-active routing via CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH) with a single multi-tenant DB keyed by a row-level engagement_id — the production-architecture baseline and the practical enabler of the Agent Profile Registry's cross-engagement learning (DEC-373). The Architecture phase had to settle three genuinely contestable choices before any migration code: how to merge two files' identifier sequences without renumbering history (CRMBUILDER is at SES-150, CBM at SES-001, both hold a row 'SES-001'), how the access layer selects an engagement once it is a filter rather than a file, and whether the engagement registry stays a separate store.
decisions.decision: (1) Fold the meta DB into the unified DB — the engagements table becomes the in-DB tenant registry and engagement_id is its surrogate integer PK; retire the second engine and the meta Alembic chain. (2) Add engagement_id NOT NULL FK to the ~30 governance + methodology tables; keep the catalog_* base-entity-catalog tables system/shared (no column) — the precedent PI-122's registry reuses (system scope = un-scoped / NULL). (3) Resolve the identifier collision with composite UNIQUE(engagement_id, identifier), preserving every engagement's existing sequence verbatim (no history renumbered); identifier_reservations and refs.reference_identifier gain engagement_id too. (4) Rewrite the access layer with a central mechanism — an active-engagement ContextVar, a do_orm_execute event applying with_loader_criteria(engagement_id == active) to every scoped mapped class for reads, and a before_flush event stamping engagement_id on new scoped rows — NOT a threaded engagement_id parameter; an unset-context guard fails loud, and an enumerated audit list covers ORM-bypass sites (raw SQL, PK gets, the exporter, the change-log emitter, the next-identifier helpers). (5) Resolve the active engagement per request from an X-Engagement header, falling back to the current_engagement.json marker so every existing single-active workflow keeps working; retire route_settings_to_engagement / CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH file-routing. Data migration is a one-shot idempotent consolidation of the live CRMBUILDER.db + CBM.db (add-nullable -> backfill -> enforce), validated by a cross-engagement leak-test. Full detail in pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Access-layer rewrite: thread an engagement_id argument through every repository function and router — rejected because the blast radius (~35 repositories + every call site) is far larger and equally error-prone, and it makes the default dangerous (a forgotten filter leaks all engagements) where the central mechanism makes the default correct; the cost is the filter being invisible at the call site, accepted and pinned by the leak-test. Identifier collision: renumber one engagement's history on merge — rejected (destroys auditability, breaks every external reference to SES-NNN/DEC-NNN). Keep the meta DB separate in the unified world — rejected (a second engine + chain is pure overhead once there is one file; the awkward two-database API server goes away). Catalog per-engagement — rejected (duplicates ~10 reference tables x N engagements; the shared bucket is the registry's system-scope precedent).
decisions.consequences: PI-123 decomposes into the six ADO phase Workstreams (Architecture complete; Development 5 slices; Testing the leak-test; Data Migration; Documentation; Deployment). The build is rework-free for PI-122: the system/shared bucket + the ContextVar stamp/filter + the composite-identifier pattern are exactly what the scope-aware registry plugs into. The design stays on SQLite but uses no SQLite-only tricks beyond batch-mode DDL, keeping a future Postgres port open. Open hardening items (WAL/connection-pool review now that writers serialize across all engagements in one file; an optional path-prefix addressing form; a system-snapshot of shared tables) are deferred. Realizes the v0.5 architecture's committed prototype-to-production migration (PI-017 / DEC-081).
decisions.executive_summary: Settles PI-123's unified multi-engagement-DB architecture: fold the meta DB into one DB (engagements as the tenant table); add engagement_id NOT NULL to ~30 scoped tables while keeping catalog system/shared; fix the identifier-collision crux with composite UNIQUE(engagement_id, identifier) preserving every engagement's sequence untouched; rewrite the access layer with a central ContextVar + do_orm_execute read-filter + before_flush stamp (central, not threaded params — default-correct, leak-test-pinned); resolve the active engagement from an X-Engagement header with the current_engagement.json marker default, retiring CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH routing. Decomposes into the six ADO phase Workstreams and leaves the hooks PI-122's scope-aware registry plugs into.
